mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical correlation with FEDFUNDS changes (Rank IC < 0.02) across all tested periods.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that immediately trigger risk-off positioning, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns remain positive in > 55% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.2pp.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand strength that sustains corporate earnings growth, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day returns (logistic regression coefficient p-value > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production growth during periods of high capacity utilization reflects tightening supply constraints that boost corporate pricing power and profit margins, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration produces negative or flat 20-day returns during late-cycle expansions, with out-of-sample accuracy below 52%.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real gross domestic product directly translates to higher aggregate corporate revenues, creating a persistent positive drift in S&P 500 prices that manifests over the next 5 trading days following data release.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show zero predictive power for 5-day forward returns (Rank IC consistently near zero across rolling windows).

mechanism_narrative: Sustained consumer price index growth above 3.0% erodes real purchasing power and forces monetary tightening expectations, triggering immediate equity deleveraging and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in > 60% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds 3.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising total nonfarm payrolls and falling unemployment rates indicates robust labor market absorption that supports consumer spending and corporate earnings, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The PAYEMS-UNRATE divergence signal yields a negative Sharpe ratio over 20-day holding periods in backtests.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp month-over-month contractions in real personal consumption expenditures during recessionary periods signal collapsing consumer demand that immediately depresses equity valuations, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the following 5 trading days.
failure_condition: RSAFS contractions fail to predict negative 5-day returns during recessions, with directional accuracy below 45%.
